# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
  agent any

  options {
    timestamps()
    ansiColor('xterm')
    disableConcurrentBuilds()
    buildDiscarder(logRotator(numToKeepStr: '30', artifactNumToKeepStr: '10'))
  }

  parameters {
    choice(
      name: 'SHELL',
      choices: ['bash', 'sh'],
      description: 'Shell to use for executing commands.'
    )

    booleanParam(
      name: 'FAIL_ON_ERROR',
      defaultValue: true,
      description: 'If true, any command failure fails the build. If false, failures are recorded but pipeline continues.'
    )

    booleanParam(
      name: 'RUN_BUILD',
      defaultValue: true,
      description: 'Run build stage.'
    )
    text(
      name: 'BUILD_COMMANDS',
      defaultValue: 'echo "No build commands provided"\n',
      description: 'Build commands to run (multi-line).'
    )

    booleanParam(
      name: 'RUN_TESTS',
      defaultValue: true,
      description: 'Run test stage.'
    )
    text(
      name: 'TEST_COMMANDS',
      defaultValue: 'echo "No test commands provided"\n',
      description: 'Test commands to run (multi-line).'
    )

    booleanParam(
      name: 'RUN_DEPLOY',
      defaultValue: false,
      description: 'Run deploy stage.'
    )
    text(
      name: 'DEPLOY_COMMANDS',
      defaultValue: 'echo "No deploy commands provided"\n',
      description: 'Deployment commands to run (multi-line).'
    )

    booleanParam(
      name: 'DRY_RUN',
      defaultValue: false,
      description: 'If true, prints commands instead of executing them.'
    )

    booleanParam(
      name: 'PRINT_ENV',
      defaultValue: false,
      description: 'If true, prints environment variables (sanitized).'
    )

    string(
      name: 'WORKDIR',
      defaultValue: '',
      description: 'Optional working directory to run commands in (relative to workspace). Leave empty for workspace root.'
    )
  }

  environment {
    PIPELINE_FAIL_COUNT = '0'
  }

  stages {
    stage('Checkout') {
      steps {
        checkout scm
      }
    }

    stage('Info') {
      steps {
        script {
          if (params.PRINT_ENV) {
            def safeEnv = env.getEnvironment().collectEntries { k, v ->
              def key = k?.toString() ?: ''
              def val = v?.toString() ?: ''
              if (key.toLowerCase().contains('password') ||
                  key.toLowerCase().contains('secret') ||
                  key.toLowerCase().contains('token') ||
                  key.toLowerCase().contains('key')) {
                [(key): '****']
              } else {
                [(key): val]
              }
            }
            echo safeEnv.collect { k, v -> "${k}=${v}" }.sort().join('\n')
          }
        }
      }
    }

    stage('Build') {
      when { expression { return params.RUN_BUILD } }
      steps {
        script { runUserCommands('Build', params.BUILD_COMMANDS) }
      }
    }

    stage('Tests') {
      when { expression { return params.RUN_TESTS } }
      steps {
        script { runUserCommands('Tests', params.TEST_COMMANDS) }
      }
    }

    stage('Deploy') {
      when { expression { return params.RUN_DEPLOY } }
      steps {
        script { runUserCommands('Deploy', params.DEPLOY_COMMANDS) }
      }
    }
  }

  post {
    always {
      script {
        def fails = (env.PIPELINE_FAIL_COUNT ?: '0') as Integer
        if (fails > 0) {
          currentBuild.result = currentBuild.result ?: 'UNSTABLE'
          echo "Completed with ${fails} command failure(s)."
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

def runUserCommands(String stageName, String commandsText) {
  def shell = (params.SHELL ?: 'bash').toString().trim()
  if (!['bash', 'sh'].contains(shell)) {
    error("Unsupported SHELL: ${shell}. Allowed: bash, sh")
  }

  def cmds = (commandsText ?: '').toString().replace('\r\n', '\n').trim()
  if (!cmds) {
    echo "${stageName}: No commands provided."
    return
  }

  def workdir = (params.WORKDIR ?: '').toString().trim()
  def execBody = {
    def scriptText = buildShellScript(shell, cmds, params.FAIL_ON_ERROR as boolean, params.DRY_RUN as boolean)
    if (shell == 'bash') {
      sh(label: "${stageName} (bash)", script: scriptText)
    } else {
      sh(label: "${stageName} (sh)", script: scriptText)
    }
  }

  if (workdir) {
    dir(workdir) {
      execBody()
    }
  } else {
    execBody()
  }
}

def buildShellScript(String shell, String userCommands, boolean failOnError, boolean dryRun) {
  def header = []
  if (shell == 'bash') {
    header << '#!/usr/bin/env bash'
    header << 'set -o pipefail'
  } else {
    header << '#!/bin/sh'
  }

  header << 'echo "=== Running user-provided commands ==="'
  header << 'echo "Workspace: ${PWD}"'
  header << 'echo "Node: ${NODE_NAME:-unknown}"'
  header << 'echo "Job: ${JOB_NAME:-unknown}"'
  header << 'echo "Build: ${BUILD_NUMBER:-unknown}"'
  header << 'echo "====================================="'

  def body = []
  if (dryRun) {
    body << 'echo "--- DRY RUN: commands below would be executed ---"'
    body << 'cat <<\'__USER_CMDS__\''
    body << userCommands
    body << '__USER_CMDS__'
    body << 'echo "--- END DRY RUN ---"'
  } else {
    if (failOnError) {
      if (shell == 'bash') {
        body << 'set -euo pipefail'
      } else {
        body << 'set -eu'
      }
      body << userCommands
    } else {
      body << 'set +e'
      body << 'FAILS=0'
      body << 'run_block() {'
      body << '  echo "----- COMMAND BLOCK START -----"'
      body << '  ('
      body << userCommands
      body << '  )'
      body << '  RC=$?'
      body << '  echo "----- COMMAND BLOCK END (rc=$RC) -----"'
      body << '  if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then FAILS=$((FAILS+1)); fi'
      body << '  return 0'
      body << '}'
      body << 'run_block'
      body << 'echo "Non-fatal failures in this stage: $FAILS"'
      body << 'if [ "$FAILS" -ne 0 ]; then echo "Marking build UNSTABLE due to non-fatal failures."; fi'
      body << 'exit 0'
    }
  }

  def scriptText = (header + [''] + body).join('\n') + '\n'

  if (!failOnError && !dryRun) {
    // Capture failures count by parsing output is brittle; instead, increment a pipeline env flag if any non-zero occurs.
    // We do this by re-running the block with return code capture in Jenkins step:
    // Not possible here; keep pipeline-level counter via try/catch around sh.
    // Implemented below by wrapping sh call in runUserCommands.
  }

  return scriptText
}

def sh(Map args) {
  try {
    return steps.sh(args)
  } catch (Throwable t) {
    if (!(params.FAIL_ON_ERROR as boolean)) {
      def fails = (env.PIPELINE_FAIL_COUNT ?: '0') as Integer
      env.PIPELINE_FAIL_COUNT = (fails + 1).toString()
      echo "Command failure ignored (FAIL_ON_ERROR=false): ${t.getMessage()}"
      return null
    }
    throw t
  }
}
